question.english: Do you think the internet is a good place to learn about history?
question.chinese: 主題：你認為互聯網是一個學習歷史的好地方嗎？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think the internet is a good place to learn about history?
主題：你認為互聯網是一個學習歷史的好地方嗎？

components.english: Yes, I do think the internet can be a very good place to learn about history, because it puts an enormous amount of information right at people’s fingertips.
components.chinese: 是的，我的確認為互聯網可以是一個很好的歷史學習平台，因為它把大量資訊直接放到人們觸手可及的地方。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Yes, I do think the internet can be a very good place to learn about
history, because it puts an enormous amount of information right at people’s
fingertips.
[是的，我的確認為互聯網可以是一個很好的歷史學習平台，因為它把大量資訊直接放到人
們觸手可及的地方。]

components.english: The main reason is that it makes history far more accessible than it used to be. In the past, people often had to rely on textbooks, libraries, or formal classes, but now they can explore museum websites, watch historical documentaries, listen to expert talks, and even look at old photographs, maps, or original documents online. In that sense, the internet can breathe life into the past. It stops history from feeling like a dusty shelf of dates and turns it into something more vivid, layered, and human.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，它令歷史比以前容易接觸得多。過去，人們往往只能依靠課本、圖書館，或者正式課堂去學習；但現在，他們可以瀏覽博物館網站、看歷史紀錄片、聽專家講座，甚至在網上查看舊照片、地圖，或者原始文獻。從這個角度來說，互聯網能為過去注入生命。它不再讓歷史只像一排佈滿灰塵的年份和事件，而是把它變成一種更鮮活、更有層次、也更有人味的東西。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that it makes history far more accessible
than it used to be. In the past, people often had to rely on textbooks, libraries,
or formal classes, but now they can explore museum websites, watch historical
documentaries, listen to expert talks, and even look at old photographs, maps, or
original documents online. In that sense, the internet can breathe life into the
past. It stops history from feeling like a dusty shelf of dates and turns it into
something more vivid, layered, and human.
[主要原因是，它令歷史比以前容易接觸得多。過去，人們往往只能依靠課本、圖書館，或者
正式課堂去學習；但現在，他們可以瀏覽博物館網站、看歷史紀錄片、聽專家講座，甚至在
網上查看舊照片、地圖，或者原始文獻。從這個角度來說，互聯網能為過去注入生命。它不
再讓歷史只像一排佈滿灰塵的年份和事件，而是把它變成一種更鮮活、更有層次、也更有人
味的東西。]

components.english: For example, if a student wants to learn about the Second World War or ancient China, they do not have to rely on one short chapter in a school book. They can compare different sources, watch a virtual museum tour, listen to a historian explain the background, and see images of real artefacts or historical sites. That kind of access can make learning much more engaging.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一位學生想了解第二次世界大戰，或者中國古代歷史，他不需要只依賴課本裡短短的一章內容。他可以比較不同資料來源、觀看虛擬博物館導覽、聽歷史學者解說背景，還可以看到真實文物或歷史遺址的圖片。這種接觸方式，能令學習變得吸引得多。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a student wants to learn about the Second World
War or ancient China, they do not have to rely on one short chapter in a school
book. They can compare different sources, watch a virtual museum tour, listen
to a historian explain the background, and see images of real artefacts or
historical sites. That kind of access can make learning much more engaging.
[例如，如果一位學生想了解第二次世界大戰，或者中國古代歷史，他不需要只依賴課本裡
短短的一章內容。他可以比較不同資料來源、觀看虛擬博物館導覽、聽歷史學者解說背景，
還可以看到真實文物或歷史遺址的圖片。這種接觸方式，能令學習變得吸引得多。]

components.english: So yes, I would say the internet is a very useful place to learn about history, especially as a starting point, because it opens doors that used to be much harder to reach.
components.chinese: 所以，是的，我會說互聯網是一個很有用的歷史學習地方，尤其作為入門起點時更是如此，因為它打開了很多以前不容易接觸到的大門。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So yes, I would say the internet is a very useful place to learn
about history, especially as a starting point, because it opens doors that used to
be much harder to reach.
[所以，是的，我會說互聯網是一個很有用的歷史學習地方，尤其作為入門起點時更是如此
，因為它打開了很多以前不容易接觸到的大門。]

components.english: That said, I do not think it is a good place to learn history blindly, because not everything online is reliable.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為人們可以毫無保留地依賴網絡學歷史，因為網上的資訊並不是全部都可靠。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think it is a good place to learn history blindly,
because not everything online is reliable.
[不過，我也不認為人們可以毫無保留地依賴網絡學歷史，因為網上的資訊並不是全部都可

components.english: To be honest, the internet is a gold mine, but it can also be a minefield. There is a huge difference between a museum archive, an academic article, and a random post that sounds confident but gets the facts wrong. History is especially sensitive to bias, oversimplification, and one-sided storytelling, so if people only skim the surface or believe the first thing they read, they can end up learning a version of history that is shaky at best and misleading at worst. In other words, the internet is a powerful tool, but it needs to be handled with care.
components.chinese: 老實說，互聯網像一座金礦，但同時也可能像一片地雷區。博物館檔案、學術文章，和一些聽起來很有自信、但其實內容錯漏百出的隨意貼文，三者之間其實有很大分別。歷史這個題目本身特別容易受到偏見、過度簡化，以及單一敘事的影響，所以如果人們只是看表面，或者讀到第一個答案就照單全收，那最後學到的歷史版本，輕則不夠穩固，重則可能完全誤導。換句話說，互聯網是一個很有力的工具，但一定要小心使用。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, the internet is a gold mine, but it can also be a
minefield. There is a huge difference between a museum archive, an academic
article, and a random post that sounds confident but gets the facts wrong.
History is especially sensitive to bias, oversimplification, and one-sided
storytelling, so if people only skim the surface or believe the first thing they
read, they can end up learning a version of history that is shaky at best and
misleading at worst. In other words, the internet is a powerful tool, but it needs
to be handled with care.
[老實說，互聯網像一座金礦，但同時也可能像一片地雷區。博物館檔案、學術文章，和一些
聽起來很有自信、但其實內容錯漏百出的隨意貼文，三者之間其實有很大分別。歷史這個題
目本身特別容易受到偏見、過度簡化，以及單一敘事的影響，所以如果人們只是看表面，或
者讀到第一個答案就照單全收，那最後學到的歷史版本，輕則不夠穩固，重則可能完全誤
導。換句話說，互聯網是一個很有力的工具，但一定要小心使用。]

components.english: For instance, if someone searches for a controversial historical event, they may quickly come across videos or articles that leave out key context or push a particular agenda. However, if they check reputable sources, compare viewpoints, and look for evidence, the internet can still be incredibly valuable.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人搜尋某個具爭議性的歷史事件，他很快就可能看到一些省略關鍵背景，或者帶有特定立場的影片和文章。不過，如果他懂得查閱可信來源、比較不同觀點，以及尋找證據，那麼互聯網仍然可以是非常有價值的。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, if someone searches for a controversial historical
event, they may quickly come across videos or articles that leave out key context
or push a particular agenda. However, if they check reputable sources, compare
viewpoints, and look for evidence, the internet can still be incredibly valuable.

components.english: Overall, I think the internet is a very good place to learn about history, but only if people use it critically and choose their sources wisely. It can open a window to the past, but people still need clear eyes to see through that window properly.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我認為互聯網確實是一個很好的歷史學習地方，但前提是人們要有批判思考，並且懂得明智地選擇資料來源。它的確能打開一扇通往過去的窗，但人們仍然需要清醒的眼光，才能真正透過那扇窗看清楚歷史。

question.english: What is the effect of technology on how people learn about history?
question.chinese: 科技對人們學習歷史的方式有甚麼影響？

source_lines.text: Topic: What is the effect of technology on how people learn about history?
科技對人們學習歷史的方式有甚麼影響？

components.english: Overall, I think technology has changed the way people learn about history for the better, because it has made history far more accessible, vivid, and engaging than it used to be.
components.chinese: 整體來說，我認為科技確實讓人們學習歷史的方式變得更好了，因為它令歷史比以前更容易接觸、更生動，也更吸引人。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Overall, I think technology has changed the way people learn about
history for the better, because it has made history far more accessible, vivid, and
engaging than it used to be.
[整體來說，我認為科技確實讓人們學習歷史的方式變得更好了，因為它令歷史比以前更容
易接觸、更生動，也更吸引人。]

components.english: In the past, learning history often meant reading textbooks and memorising dates, which could feel dry and distant. Now, with documentaries, podcasts, online archives, interactive maps, and even virtual museum tours, history can be brought to life in a much more immediate way. Instead of just seeing the past as a list of facts, people can begin to see it as a story full of people, conflict, change, and consequences. In that sense, technology opens a window into the past and makes it easier for learners to connect the dots.
components.chinese: 以前，學習歷史通常意味著讀課本和背日期，這樣很容易讓人覺得枯燥而遙遠。現在，有了紀錄片、播客、網上檔案、互動地圖，甚至虛擬博物館導覽，歷史就能以更直接、更有臨場感的方式呈現出來。人們不再只是把過去看成一堆事實清單，而是開始把它看成一個充滿人物、衝突、變化和後果的故事。從這個角度來說，科技就像替人打開了一扇通往過去的窗，也讓學習者更容易把不同資訊串連起來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In the past, learning history often meant reading textbooks and
memorising dates, which could feel dry and distant. Now, with documentaries,
podcasts, online archives, interactive maps, and even virtual museum tours,
history can be brought to life in a much more immediate way. Instead of just
seeing the past as a list of facts, people can begin to see it as a story full of
people, conflict, change, and consequences. In that sense, technology opens a
window into the past and makes it easier for learners to connect the dots.
[以前，學習歷史通常意味著讀課本和背日期，這樣很容易讓人覺得枯燥而遙遠。現在，有
了紀錄片、播客、網上檔案、互動地圖，甚至虛擬博物館導覽，歷史就能以更直接、更有臨場
感的方式呈現出來。人們不再只是把過去看成一堆事實清單，而是開始把它看成一個充滿
人物、衝突、變化和後果的故事。從這個角度來說，科技就像替人打開了一扇通往過去的窗
，也讓學習者更容易把不同資訊串連起來。]

components.english: For example, a student who is learning about the Second World War can now watch archived footage, listen to historians explain key events, and explore digital maps showing how borders changed over time. That kind of experience is much richer than simply reading two pages in a textbook. It helps history step off the page and feel more real.
components.chinese: 例如，一位正在學習第二次世界大戰的學生，現在可以觀看歷史影片檔案、聆聽歷史學家講解關鍵事件，還可以透過數碼地圖了解國界如何隨時間改變。這種學習體驗比起單純閱讀課本的兩頁內容要豐富得多，也能讓歷史不再只是紙上的文字，而是變得更真實、更有畫面感。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, a student who is learning about the Second World War
can now watch archived footage, listen to historians explain key events, and
explore digital maps showing how borders changed over time. That kind of
experience is much richer than simply reading two pages in a textbook. It helps
history step off the page and feel more real.
[例如，一位正在學習第二次世界大戰的學生，現在可以觀看歷史影片檔案、聆聽歷史學家
講解關鍵事件，還可以透過數碼地圖了解國界如何隨時間改變。這種學習體驗比起單純閱
讀課本的兩頁內容要豐富得多，也能讓歷史不再只是紙上的文字，而是變得更真實、更有畫
面感。]

components.english: So, on the whole, I would say technology has made history easier to access and easier to appreciate, especially for people who learn better through images, sound, and interaction rather than through memorisation alone.
components.chinese: 所以，整體而言，我會說科技令歷史變得更容易接觸，也更容易被理解和欣賞，特別是對那些透過圖像、聲音和互動學得比單靠死記更好的人來說，更是如此。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, on the whole, I would say technology has made history easier
to access and easier to appreciate, especially for people who learn better through
images, sound, and interaction rather than through memorisation alone.
[所以，整體而言，我會說科技令歷史變得更容易接觸，也更容易被理解和欣賞，特別是對
那些透過圖像、聲音和互動學得比單靠死記更好的人來說，更是如此。]

components.english: That said, I do think technology can be a double-edged sword when it comes to learning history.
components.chinese: 不過，我也認為在學習歷史這件事上，科技其實是一把雙刃劍。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do think technology can be a double-edged sword when it
comes to learning history.
[不過，我也認為在學習歷史這件事上，科技其實是一把雙刃劍。]

components.english: The problem is that online information is not always accurate, balanced, or well explained. People can easily come across oversimplified videos, biased content, or dramatic posts that turn complicated historical events into black-and-white stories. On top of that, the internet encourages fast consumption, so some learners may skim the surface instead of digging deeper. History deserves patience, but technology sometimes trains people to scroll rather than reflect.
components.chinese: 問題在於，網上的資訊並不總是準確、平衡，或者解釋得足夠完整。人們很容易接觸到過度簡化的影片、帶有偏見的內容，或者一些把複雜歷史事件說成非黑即白故事的煽情貼文。除此之外，互聯網也鼓勵快速吸收資訊，所以有些學習者可能只是停留在表面，而不會真正深入理解。歷史本來需要耐性去消化，但科技有時反而訓練人不停滑過，而不是停下來思考。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: The problem is that online information is not always accurate,
balanced, or well explained. People can easily come across oversimplified videos,
biased content, or dramatic posts that turn complicated historical events into
black-and-white stories. On top of that, the internet encourages fast
consumption, so some learners may skim the surface instead of digging deeper.
History deserves patience, but technology sometimes trains people to scroll
rather than reflect.
[問題在於，網上的資訊並不總是準確、平衡，或者解釋得足夠完整。人們很容易接觸到過
度簡化的影片、帶有偏見的內容，或者一些把複雜歷史事件說成非黑即白故事的煽情貼文。
除此之外，互聯網也鼓勵快速吸收資訊，所以有些學習者可能只是停留在表面，而不會真正
深入理解。歷史本來需要耐性去消化，但科技有時反而訓練人不停滑過，而不是停下來思

components.english: For instance, someone might watch a one-minute video about a revolution and come away thinking they understand it fully, when in reality they have only seen the tip of the iceberg. If they never check the source or look at different perspectives, their understanding can end up being shallow or even misleading.
components.chinese: 例如，有人可能看完一段一分鐘講某場革命的短片，就以為自己已經完全明白了；但實際上，他看到的可能只不過是冰山一角而已。如果他從來不查證來源，也不接觸不同觀點，那麼他的理解就很可能流於膚淺，甚至是被誤導的。

source_lines.text: 7. Example 2: For instance, someone might watch a one-minute video about a

misleading.

components.english: Overall, I’d say technology has transformed the way people learn about history by making it more dynamic and approachable, but it only works well if people use it critically. Used wisely, it can bring the past into sharp focus; used carelessly, it can blur the picture instead.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說科技的確改變了人們學習歷史的方式，令它變得更有動感，也更容易接近；不過，這一切只有在人們懂得批判性使用科技時才真正有效。若使用得當，科技可以令過去變得清晰鮮明；但若使用得草率，它反而可能把整幅歷史圖像弄得模糊不清。

question.english: First of all, why do people go on holiday?
question.chinese: 首先，為甚麼人們會去度假？

source_lines.text: Topic: First of all, why do people go on holiday?
首先，為甚麼人們會去度假？

components.english: I think the main reason people go on holiday is to take a break from the routine of daily life and recharge their batteries.
components.chinese: 我認為，人們去度假的主要原因，就是想暫時離開日常生活的規律，讓自己好好充電。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think the main reason people go on holiday is to take a break from the
routine of daily life and recharge their batteries.
[我認為，人們去度假的主要原因，就是想暫時離開日常生活的規律，讓自己好好充電。]

components.english: Everyday life can be demanding, repetitive, and mentally draining. People are often tied up with work, study, family duties, and endless little responsibilities, so after a while life can start to feel like running on a treadmill. A holiday gives them breathing room. It lets them switch off, slow down, and step away from pressure for a while. In that sense, going on holiday is not just about pleasure; it is also about recovery.
components.chinese: 日常生活很多時都很有壓力、很重複，而且相當消耗心神。人們常常被工作、學業、家庭責任，以及沒完沒了的瑣事纏住，所以過了一段時間之後，生活很容易開始像在跑步機上不停奔跑一樣。度假能給人一點喘息空間，讓人可以關掉壓力、放慢腳步，暫時離開那些負擔。從這個角度來看，去度假不只是為了享樂，同時也是為了恢復。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: Everyday life can be demanding, repetitive, and mentally
draining. People are often tied up with work, study, family duties, and endless
little responsibilities, so after a while life can start to feel like running on a
treadmill. A holiday gives them breathing room. It lets them switch off, slow
down, and step away from pressure for a while. In that sense, going on holiday is
not just about pleasure; it is also about recovery.
[日常生活很多時都很有壓力、很重複，而且相當消耗心神。人們常常被工作、學業、家庭責
任，以及沒完沒了的瑣事纏住，所以過了一段時間之後，生活很容易開始像在跑步機上不停
奔跑一樣。度假能給人一點喘息空間，讓人可以關掉壓力、放慢腳步，暫時離開那些負擔。
從這個角度來看，去度假不只是為了享樂，同時也是為了恢復。]

components.english: For example, someone who has been working long hours for months may go away for just a few days, sleep more, walk around a quieter place, and come back feeling much lighter. The holiday may be short, but it can still clear the mental fog and help them feel human again.
components.chinese: 例如，一個已經連續幾個月長時間工作的人，可能只需要離開幾天，多睡一點、到一個較安靜的地方走走，就能回來時感覺輕鬆得多。那個假期也許不長，但它仍然能把腦中的疲憊感掃走，讓人重新找回一點做人的感覺。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, someone who has been working long hours for months
may go away for just a few days, sleep more, walk around a quieter place, and
come back feeling much lighter. The holiday may be short, but it can still clear
the mental fog and help them feel human again.
[例如，一個已經連續幾個月長時間工作的人，可能只需要離開幾天，多睡一點、到一個較
安靜的地方走走，就能回來時感覺輕鬆得多。那個假期也許不長，但它仍然能把腦中的疲憊
感掃走，讓人重新找回一點做人的感覺。]

components.english: So, one major reason people go on holiday is simply to rest, recover, and get away from it all for a while.
components.chinese: 所以，人們去度假其中一個很主要的原因，其實就是想休息、恢復，並且暫時離開一切繁瑣。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one major reason people go on holiday is simply to rest,
recover, and get away from it all for a while.
[所以，人們去度假其中一個很主要的原因，其實就是想休息、恢復，並且暫時離開一切繁

components.english: Another important reason is that holidays give people a chance to enjoy new experiences and spend meaningful time with others.
components.chinese: 另一個重要原因是，假期能讓人有機會接觸新體驗，也能和別人共度有意義的時光。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another important reason is that holidays give people a chance to
enjoy new experiences and spend meaningful time with others.
[另一個重要原因是，假期能讓人有機會接觸新體驗，也能和別人共度有意義的時光。]

components.english: A holiday can open a window onto a different way of living. People may see new places, try unfamiliar food, learn about another culture, or simply do things they never have time to do at home. At the same time, holidays often bring families, couples, or friends closer together, because they are finally able to share time without constantly watching the clock. In other words, a holiday is not only an escape from routine; it is also a chance to make memories that outlast the trip itself.
components.chinese: 假期很多時都像打開了一扇窗，讓人看見另一種生活方式。人們可能會去看新的地方、嘗試陌生食物、接觸另一種文化，或者單純去做一些平日在家根本沒有時間做的事。與此同時，假期往往也能拉近家人、伴侶，或朋友之間的距離，因為他們終於可以一起分享時間，而不用一直看著時鐘過日子。換句話說，假期不只是逃離日常，它同時也是創造回憶的機會，而那些回憶很多時候會比旅程本身留得更久。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: A holiday can open a window onto a different way of living.
People may see new places, try unfamiliar food, learn about another culture, or
simply do things they never have time to do at home. At the same time, holidays
often bring families, couples, or friends closer together, because they are finally
able to share time without constantly watching the clock. In other words, a
holiday is not only an escape from routine; it is also a chance to make memories
that outlast the trip itself.
[假期很多時都像打開了一扇窗，讓人看見另一種生活方式。人們可能會去看新的地方、嘗
試陌生食物、接觸另一種文化，或者單純去做一些平日在家根本沒有時間做的事。與此同時
，假期往往也能拉近家人、伴侶，或朋友之間的距離，因為他們終於可以一起分享時間，而
不用一直看著時鐘過日子。換句話說，假期不只是逃離日常，它同時也是創造回憶的機會，
而那些回憶很多時候會比旅程本身留得更久。]

components.english: For instance, a family trip may not be perfect from start to finish, but years later people often still remember the meals they shared, the places they visited, and the funny little moments along the way. Sometimes the suitcase is unpacked in a day, but the memory stays unpacked in the mind for years.
components.chinese: 例如，一次家庭旅行也許未必由頭到尾都完美無缺，但過了很多年之後，人們往往仍然會記得一起吃過的飯、一起去過的地方，以及旅途中那些有趣的小片段。有時候，行李一天之內就可以收拾好，但那些回憶卻會在心裡攤開很多年。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a family trip may not be perfect from start to finish,
but years later people often still remember the meals they shared, the places they
visited, and the funny little moments along the way. Sometimes the suitcase is
unpacked in a day, but the memory stays unpacked in the mind for years.
[例如，一次家庭旅行也許未必由頭到尾都完美無缺，但過了很多年之後，人們往往仍然會
記得一起吃過的飯、一起去過的地方，以及旅途中那些有趣的小片段。有時候，行李一天之
內就可以收拾好，但那些回憶卻會在心裡攤開很多年。]

components.english: Overall, I would say people go on holiday for two main reasons: to rest and to live a little more fully. A holiday allows people to step out of the daily grind and reconnect with the world, with other people, and with themselves.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說人們去度假主要有兩個原因：一是休息，二是讓自己活得更完整一點。假期能讓人從日常忙碌中抽身，重新和世界、他人，以及自己建立連結。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I would say people go on holiday for two main reasons:
to rest and to live a little more fully. A holiday allows people to step out of the
daily grind and reconnect with the world, with other people, and with
themselves.
[總結來說，我會說人們去度假主要有兩個原因：一是休息，二是讓自己活得更完整一
點。假期能讓人從日常忙碌中抽身，重新和世界、他人，以及自己建立連結。]

question.english: How important is it for families to go on holiday together?
question.chinese: 家人一起去旅行有多重要？

source_lines.text: Topic: How important is it for families to go on holiday together?
家人一起去旅行有多重要？

components.english: I’d say it is very important, because going on holiday together gives families time to reconnect in a way that everyday life often does not.
components.chinese: 我會說，這是非常重要的，因為一家人一起去旅行，能讓他們以一種日常生活往往做不到的方式重新建立連結。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say it is very important, because going on holiday together gives
families time to reconnect in a way that everyday life often does not.
[我會說，這是非常重要的，因為一家人一起去旅行，能讓他們以一種日常生活往往做不到
的方式重新建立連結。]

components.english: The main reason is that daily life can easily scatter family members in different directions. Parents are busy with work, children are tied up with school, and everyone ends up living under the same roof but running on different tracks. A holiday can press pause on that routine. It gives people room to talk, laugh, eat together, and actually enjoy one another’s company without always watching the clock. In that sense, a family holiday is not just a trip; it is more like a reset button for relationships.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，日常生活很容易把一家人推向不同方向。父母忙於工作，孩子忙於上學，結果大家雖然住在同一個屋簷下，卻像各自在不同軌道上前進。一次旅行可以暫時按下這種生活節奏的暫停鍵。它讓人有空間聊天、一起笑、一起吃飯，也能在不用時時看時間的情況下，真正享受彼此陪伴。從這個角度來看，家庭旅行不只是一趟外出，更像是替家庭關係按下重整鍵。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that daily life can easily scatter family
members in different directions. Parents are busy with work, children are tied
up with school, and everyone ends up living under the same roof but running
on different tracks. A holiday can press pause on that routine. It gives people
room to talk, laugh, eat together, and actually enjoy one another’s company
without always watching the clock. In that sense, a family holiday is not just a
trip; it is more like a reset button for relationships.
[主要原因是，日常生活很容易把一家人推向不同方向。父母忙於工作，孩子忙於上學，結
果大家雖然住在同一個屋簷下，卻像各自在不同軌道上前進。一次旅行可以暫時按下這種
生活節奏的暫停鍵。它讓人有空間聊天、一起笑、一起吃飯，也能在不用時時看時間的情況
下，真正享受彼此陪伴。從這個角度來看，家庭旅行不只是一趟外出，更像是替家庭關係按
下重整鍵。]

components.english: For example, if a family goes away for a few days, they may visit new places during the day, then sit down for dinner and talk more naturally than they usually do at home. Even simple things, like taking photos together or getting lost and laughing about it later, can turn into shared memories that stay with people for years.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一家人外出幾天，他們白天也許會一起去新地方，到了晚上又能坐下來吃飯，自然而然地聊更多平時在家未必會聊到的事情。即使只是一些很簡單的事，例如一起拍照，或者一度迷路之後事後笑著回想，也可能會變成多年之後仍然記得的共同回憶。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if a family goes away for a few days, they may visit new
places during the day, then sit down for dinner and talk more naturally than
they usually do at home. Even simple things, like taking photos together or
getting lost and laughing about it later, can turn into shared memories that stay
with people for years.
[例如，如果一家人外出幾天，他們白天也許會一起去新地方，到了晚上又能坐下來吃飯，
自然而然地聊更多平時在家未必會聊到的事情。即使只是一些很簡單的事，例如一起拍照，
或者一度迷路之後事後笑著回想，也可能會變成多年之後仍然記得的共同回憶。]

components.english: So, to me, family holidays are important because they create quality time, strengthen emotional bonds, and give people memories to hold on to.
components.chinese: 所以，對我來說，家庭旅行之所以重要，是因為它能創造有質素的相處時間、加深情感連結，也讓人留下可以珍惜的回憶。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, to me, family holidays are important because they create
quality time, strengthen emotional bonds, and give people memories to hold on
to.
[所以，對我來說，家庭旅行之所以重要，是因為它能創造有質素的相處時間、加深情感連
結，也讓人留下可以珍惜的回憶。]

components.english: That said, I do not think the value lies only in going far away or spending a lot of money, because what really matters is being together, not just changing locations.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為它的價值只在於去得多遠，或者花多少錢，因為真正重要的，其實是一起相處，而不只是換了一個地方。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think the value lies only in going far away or
spending a lot of money, because what really matters is being together, not just
changing locations.
[不過，我也不認為它的價值只在於去得多遠，或者花多少錢，因為真正重要的，其實是一
起相處，而不只是換了一個地方。]

components.english: To be honest, not every family holiday is picture-perfect. Travel can be expensive, tiring, and sometimes stressful, especially if people argue or have very different expectations. So I would not romanticise it too much. Still, even a short break can do wonders if the atmosphere is right. A weekend trip, a staycation, or even a simple day out can bring people closer, provided that they are genuinely present with one another. In other words, it is not about the postcard view; it is about the human view.
components.chinese: 老實說，並不是每一次家庭旅行都像照片裡那麼完美。旅行可能很花錢、很累，有時甚至會有壓力，尤其當大家意見不同，或者對旅程有不同期待時。所以，我也不會把它想得過分浪漫。不過，只要氣氛對了，即使只是短短的休息，也可能很有幫助。一次週末小旅行、一次本地 staycation，甚至只是簡單的一日遊，都可以讓彼此更親近，前提是大家真的把心放在彼此身上。換句話說，重點不在於明信片般的風景，而在於人與人之間的風景。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: To be honest, not every family holiday is picture-perfect.
Travel can be expensive, tiring, and sometimes stressful, especially if people
argue or have very different expectations. So I would not romanticise it too
much. Still, even a short break can do wonders if the atmosphere is right. A
weekend trip, a staycation, or even a simple day out can bring people closer,
provided that they are genuinely present with one another. In other words, it is
not about the postcard view; it is about the human view.
[老實說，並不是每一次家庭旅行都像照片裡那麼完美。旅行可能很花錢、很累，有時甚至
會有壓力，尤其當大家意見不同，或者對旅程有不同期待時。所以，我也不會把它想得過分
浪漫。不過，只要氣氛對了，即使只是短短的休息，也可能很有幫助。一次週末小旅行、一次
本地 staycation，甚至只是簡單的一日遊，都可以讓彼此更親近，前提是大家真的把心放在
彼此身上。換句話說，重點不在於明信片般的風景，而在於人與人之間的風景。]

components.english: For instance, a family does not necessarily need to fly abroad to benefit from time together. They might spend one weekend by the beach, go hiking, or stay in a hotel locally and simply enjoy uninterrupted time as a family. If everyone comes back feeling a little warmer towards each other, then the holiday has already done its job.
components.chinese: 例如，一個家庭其實不一定要飛到外國，才能從相處時間中得到好處。他們可以只是找個週末去海邊、去行山，或者在本地住一晚酒店，單純享受不被打擾的家庭時光。如果大家回來之後，彼此之間多了一點溫度，那這趟旅程其實就已經發揮了它的作用。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, a family does not necessarily need to fly abroad to
benefit from time together. They might spend one weekend by the beach, go
hiking, or stay in a hotel locally and simply enjoy uninterrupted time as a family.
If everyone comes back feeling a little warmer towards each other, then the
holiday has already done its job.
[例如，一個家庭其實不一定要飛到外國，才能從相處時間中得到好處。他們可以只是找個
週末去海邊、去行山，或者在本地住一晚酒店，單純享受不被打擾的家庭時光。如果大家回
來之後，彼此之間多了一點溫度，那這趟旅程其實就已經發揮了它的作用。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say it is very important for families to go on holiday together, but the heart of it is connection rather than cost or distance. A good family holiday does not have to be grand; it just has to bring people back to one another.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說家人一起去旅行是很重要的，不過它最核心的價值在於連結，而不是花費多少，或者去得多遠。一趟好的家庭旅行不一定要很盛大；它只需要把彼此重新帶回彼此身邊。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say it is very important for families to go on holiday
together, but the heart of it is connection rather than cost or distance. A good
family holiday does not have to be grand; it just has to bring people back to one
another.
[總結來說，我會說家人一起去旅行是很重要的，不過它最核心的價值在於連結，而不是花
費多少，或者去得多遠。一趟好的家庭旅行不一定要很盛大；它只需要把彼此重新帶回彼此
身邊。]

question.english: Why do some people go on holiday alone?
question.chinese: 主題：為甚麼有些人會獨自去旅行？

source_lines.text: Topic: Why do some people go on holiday alone?
[主題：為甚麼有些人會獨自去旅行？]

components.english: I think one major reason is freedom, because travelling alone allows people to do exactly what they want, at exactly the pace that suits them.
components.chinese: 我認為其中一個主要原因是自由，因為獨自旅行可以讓人按照自己真正想要的方式，以及最適合自己的節奏去行動。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think one major reason is freedom, because travelling alone allows
people to do exactly what they want, at exactly the pace that suits them.
[我認為其中一個主要原因是自由，因為獨自旅行可以讓人按照自己真正想要的方式，以及
最適合自己的節奏去行動。]

components.english: When people travel with others, they often have to compromise on timing, budget, food, activities, and even how long they stay in one place. By contrast, a solo trip means no tug-of-war over plans and no need to keep checking whether everyone is happy. If someone wants to wake up late, wander around for hours, or change course on a whim, they can. In that sense, travelling alone can feel like breathing with your full lungs for once.
components.chinese: 當人們和別人一起旅行時，他們往往需要在時間、預算、飲食、活動，甚至逗留多久這些事情上互相遷就。相比之下，獨自旅行就不用在行程上來回拉扯，也不需要不停確認每一個人是否都滿意。如果一個人想晚起、隨意走上幾個小時，或者突然改變路線，他都可以直接這樣做。從這個角度來說，獨自旅行有時就像終於能夠好好呼吸一口完整的氣。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: When people travel with others, they often have to compromise
on timing, budget, food, activities, and even how long they stay in one place. By
contrast, a solo trip means no tug-of-war over plans and no need to keep
checking whether everyone is happy. If someone wants to wake up late, wander
around for hours, or change course on a whim, they can. In that sense, travelling
alone can feel like breathing with your full lungs for once.
[當人們和別人一起旅行時，他們往往需要在時間、預算、飲食、活動，甚至逗留多久這些事
情上互相遷就。相比之下，獨自旅行就不用在行程上來回拉扯，也不需要不停確認每一個人
是否都滿意。如果一個人想晚起、隨意走上幾個小時，或者突然改變路線，他都可以直接這
樣做。從這個角度來說，獨自旅行有時就像終於能夠好好呼吸一口完整的氣。]

components.english: For example, if someone goes to a new city alone, they might spend half a day in a museum, sit quietly in a café, or walk through side streets without worrying that they are boring anyone else. If they feel like changing the day’s plan at the last minute, they can simply do it.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個人獨自去到一個新的城市，他可能會花半天待在博物館、安靜地坐在咖啡店裡，或者在小街小巷慢慢走，而不用擔心自己是不是令同行的人覺得無聊。如果他想在最後一刻改變當天的行程，也可以直接這樣做。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone goes to a new city alone, they might spend
half a day in a museum, sit quietly in a café, or walk through side streets
without worrying that they are boring anyone else. If they feel like changing the
day’s plan at the last minute, they can simply do it.
[例如，如果一個人獨自去到一個新的城市，他可能會花半天待在博物館、安靜地坐在咖啡
店裡，或者在小街小巷慢慢走，而不用擔心自己是不是令同行的人覺得無聊。如果他想在最
後一刻改變當天的行程，也可以直接這樣做。]

components.english: So, one clear reason people travel alone is that it gives them independence, flexibility, and the chance to enjoy the journey on their own terms.
components.chinese: 所以，人們獨自旅行其中一個很清楚的原因，就是它能帶來獨立、自主，以及按照自己方式享受旅程的機會。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, one clear reason people travel alone is that it gives them
independence, flexibility, and the chance to enjoy the journey on their own
terms.
[所以，人們獨自旅行其中一個很清楚的原因，就是它能帶來獨立、自主，以及按照自己方
式享受旅程的機會。]

components.english: Another reason is that going on holiday alone can be a very personal experience, because some people use it to clear their head, rebuild confidence, or reconnect with themselves.
components.chinese: 另一個原因是，獨自旅行可以是一種非常個人的體驗，因為有些人會藉此清空思緒、重建自信，或者重新和自己連結。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: Another reason is that going on holiday alone can be a very personal
experience, because some people use it to clear their head, rebuild confidence, or
reconnect with themselves.
[另一個原因是，獨自旅行可以是一種非常個人的體驗，因為有些人會藉此清空思緒、重建
自信，或者重新和自己連結。]

components.english: Daily life can be noisy, crowded, and emotionally draining, so for some people, being alone on holiday is not lonely at all; it is peaceful. It gives them space to think, reflect, and step outside the usual grind. Sometimes a solo trip is less about sightseeing and more about pressing reset. People may come back feeling calmer, clearer, and a little more grounded. In a way, travelling alone can act like a conversation with yourself, only with better scenery.
components.chinese: 日常生活有時很嘈雜、很擁擠，而且相當消耗情緒，所以對某些人來說，假期裡獨處一點都不代表孤單，反而代表平靜。那能給他們空間去思考、反省，並且暫時走出平日的勞碌節奏。有時一趟獨自旅行與其說是為了看風景，不如說更像是在替自己按下重啟鍵。人們回來時，往往會感到更平靜、更清晰，也更踏實一點。某程度上，獨自旅行就像和自己進行一場對話，只不過背景風景更好看。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: Daily life can be noisy, crowded, and emotionally draining, so
for some people, being alone on holiday is not lonely at all; it is peaceful. It gives
them space to think, reflect, and step outside the usual grind. Sometimes a solo
trip is less about sightseeing and more about pressing reset. People may come
back feeling calmer, clearer, and a little more grounded. In a way, travelling
alone can act like a conversation with yourself, only with better scenery.
[日常生活有時很嘈雜、很擁擠，而且相當消耗情緒，所以對某些人來說，假期裡獨處一點
都不代表孤單，反而代表平靜。那能給他們空間去思考、反省，並且暫時走出平日的勞碌節
奏。有時一趟獨自旅行與其說是為了看風景，不如說更像是在替自己按下重啟鍵。人們回來
時，往往會感到更平靜、更清晰，也更踏實一點。某程度上，獨自旅行就像和自己進行一場
對話，只不過背景風景更好看。]

components.english: For instance, someone who has been under a lot of pressure at work may choose to take a short solo trip to the countryside or to another city. They may spend the time reading, walking, eating well, and just enjoying the quiet. That kind of trip can help them come back with a lighter mind and more energy.
components.chinese: 例如，一個工作壓力很大的人，可能會選擇自己去鄉下或另一個城市短途旅行幾天。他也許會把時間花在閱讀、散步、好好吃飯，以及單純享受安靜上。這樣的一趟旅程，往往能幫助他帶着更輕鬆的心情和更多能量回來。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone who has been under a lot of pressure at work
may choose to take a short solo trip to the countryside or to another city. They
may spend the time reading, walking, eating well, and just enjoying the quiet.
That kind of trip can help them come back with a lighter mind and more
energy.
[例如，一個工作壓力很大的人，可能會選擇自己去鄉下或另一個城市短途旅行幾天。他也
許會把時間花在閱讀、散步、好好吃飯，以及單純享受安靜上。這樣的一趟旅程，往往能幫
助他帶着更輕鬆的心情和更多能量回來。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say some people go on holiday alone because they want both freedom and inner space. Of course, it is not everyone’s cup of tea, because some people prefer shared memories and company. But for the right person, a solo holiday can be not only enjoyable, but genuinely refreshing.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說有些人獨自去旅行，是因為他們同時想要自由，也想要內在的空間。當然，這並不是每個人都會喜歡的方式，因為有些人更偏好有人陪伴，以及一起創造回憶。不過，對適合的人來說，一趟獨自旅行不但可以很享受，而且真的能令人重新恢復元氣。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion: Overall, I’d say some people go on holiday alone because they
want both freedom and inner space. Of course, it is not everyone’s cup of tea,
because some people prefer shared memories and company. But for the right
person, a solo holiday can be not only enjoyable, but genuinely refreshing.
[總結來說，我會說有些人獨自去旅行，是因為他們同時想要自由，也想要內在的空間。當
然，這並不是每個人都會喜歡的方式，因為有些人更偏好有人陪伴，以及一起創造回憶。不
過，對適合的人來說，一趟獨自旅行不但可以很享受，而且真的能令人重新恢復元氣。]

question.english: How have holidays changed over the past few decades?
question.chinese: 過去幾十年來，度假方式有甚麼改變？

source_lines.text: Topic: How have holidays changed over the past few decades?
過去幾十年來，度假方式有甚麼改變？

components.english: I’d say holidays have changed quite a lot over the past few decades, mainly because they have become easier to organise, more frequent, and much more varied.
components.chinese: 我會說，過去幾十年來，度假方式其實改變了很多，主要是因為它們變得更容易安排、更頻繁，而且也多樣化了很多。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I’d say holidays have changed quite a lot over the past few decades,
mainly because they have become easier to organise, more frequent, and much
more varied.
[我會說，過去幾十年來，度假方式其實改變了很多，主要是因為它們變得更容易安排、更
頻繁，而且也多樣化了很多。]

components.english: In the past, holidays were often simpler and less flexible. People usually had fewer options, less information, and fewer cheap ways to travel. These days, however, people can book flights, hotels, and activities at the touch of a button, so travelling feels far more accessible than it used to. In a way, holidays have gone from being a big occasional event to something people can shape more freely around their time, budget, and interests.
components.chinese: 以前的假期通常比較簡單，也沒有那麼靈活。人們往往選擇較少、資訊較少，而且便宜出行的方法也沒有那麼多。不過現在，人們只要按幾下就能預訂機票、酒店和活動，所以旅行比以前容易接觸得多。某程度上，假期已經從一件偶爾才有的大事，變成一件人們可以按自己的時間、預算和興趣更自由安排的事情。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: In the past, holidays were often simpler and less flexible. People
usually had fewer options, less information, and fewer cheap ways to travel.
These days, however, people can book flights, hotels, and activities at the touch
of a button, so travelling feels far more accessible than it used to. In a way,
holidays have gone from being a big occasional event to something people can
shape more freely around their time, budget, and interests.
[以前的假期通常比較簡單，也沒有那麼靈活。人們往往選擇較少、資訊較少，而且便宜出
行的方法也沒有那麼多。不過現在，人們只要按幾下就能預訂機票、酒店和活動，所以旅行
比以前容易接觸得多。某程度上，假期已經從一件偶爾才有的大事，變成一件人們可以按自
己的時間、預算和興趣更自由安排的事情。]

components.english: For example, years ago a family might save up for one main holiday a year and go somewhere fairly predictable. Now, the same family might take a short city break, a beach holiday, or even a last-minute trip abroad, simply because planning everything is quicker and cheaper than before.
components.chinese: 例如，以前一個家庭可能會為一年一次的主要假期儲錢，然後去一個比較固定、可預測的地方。現在，同一個家庭卻可能去一次短途城市旅行、一次海灘假期，甚至來一趟臨時決定的海外之旅，因為現在規劃一切都比以前更快，也更便宜。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, years ago a family might save up for one main holiday a
year and go somewhere fairly predictable. Now, the same family might take a
short city break, a beach holiday, or even a last-minute trip abroad, simply
because planning everything is quicker and cheaper than before.
[例如，以前一個家庭可能會為一年一次的主要假期儲錢，然後去一個比較固定、可預測的
地方。現在，同一個家庭卻可能去一次短途城市旅行、一次海灘假期，甚至來一趟臨時決定
的海外之旅，因為現在規劃一切都比以前更快，也更便宜。]

components.english: So, overall, I think holidays have become more convenient, more personalised, and more within reach for ordinary people.
components.chinese: 所以，整體來說，我覺得現在的假期變得更方便、更個人化，也更是一般人負擔得起、接觸得到的。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, overall, I think holidays have become more convenient, more
personalised, and more within reach for ordinary people.
[所以，整體來說，我覺得現在的假期變得更方便、更個人化，也更是一般人負擔得起、接觸
得到的。]

components.english: That said, I also think holidays have become less restful in some ways, because modern life tends to follow people wherever they go.
components.chinese: 不過，我也覺得現在的假期在某些方面變得沒有那麼能讓人真正休息，因為現代生活往往會一路跟著人走。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I also think holidays have become less restful in some ways,
because modern life tends to follow people wherever they go.
[不過，我也覺得現在的假期在某些方面變得沒有那麼能讓人真正休息，因為現代生活往往
會一路跟著人走。]

components.english: These days, even when people are away, they are often still checking emails, replying to messages, posting photos, or worrying about getting the perfect travel experience. As a result, holidays can sometimes feel less like a genuine break and more like a change of scenery with Wi-Fi. In the past, people may have had fewer choices, but they were perhaps better able to switch off and simply enjoy where they were.
components.chinese: 現在，即使人已經去旅行，很多人仍然會查看電郵、回覆訊息、上載照片，或者煩惱怎樣才算得到一個完美的旅行體驗。結果，假期有時不像真正的休息，反而更像只是換了一個有Wi-Fi 的地方而已。以前的人雖然選擇較少，但某程度上，他們可能反而更能真正放鬆下來，好好享受自己身處的地方。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation 2: These days, even when people are away, they are often still
checking emails, replying to messages, posting photos, or worrying about
getting the perfect travel experience. As a result, holidays can sometimes feel less
like a genuine break and more like a change of scenery with Wi-Fi. In the past,
people may have had fewer choices, but they were perhaps better able to switch
off and simply enjoy where they were.
[現在，即使人已經去旅行，很多人仍然會查看電郵、回覆訊息、上載照片，或者煩惱怎樣才
算得到一個完美的旅行體驗。結果，假期有時不像真正的休息，反而更像只是換了一個有
Wi-Fi 的地方而已。以前的人雖然選擇較少，但某程度上，他們可能反而更能真正放鬆下來
，好好享受自己身處的地方。]

components.english: For instance, some people now spend half their holiday planning the next stop, taking photos for social media, or staring at their phone instead of looking at the view in front of them. In that case, they may travel far, but they do not fully arrive.
components.chinese: 例如，現在有些人旅行時，會花很多時間計劃下一站、拍照放上社交媒體，或者一直盯著手機看，而不是真正看看眼前的風景。這種情況下，他們也許走了很遠，但未必真的好好抵達了那個地方。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, some people now spend half their holiday planning
the next stop, taking photos for social media, or staring at their phone instead
of looking at the view in front of them. In that case, they may travel far, but
they do not fully arrive.
[例如，現在有些人旅行時，會花很多時間計劃下一站、拍照放上社交媒體，或者一直盯著
手機看，而不是真正看看眼前的風景。這種情況下，他們也許走了很遠，但未必真的好好抵
達了那個地方。]

components.english: Overall, I’d say holidays today are better in terms of choice and convenience, but not always better in terms of peace of mind. The world may have opened up, but truly switching off has become harder than it used to be.
components.chinese: 總結來說，我會說現在的假期在選擇和便利方面的確更好了，但在內心真正放鬆這方面卻未必一定更好。世界的確打開了，但真正把自己鬆下來，反而比以前更難。

source_lines.text: 8. Conclusion 2: Overall, I’d say holidays today are better in terms of choice and
convenience, but not always better in terms of peace of mind. The world may
have opened up, but truly switching off has become harder than it used to be.
[總結來說，我會說現在的假期在選擇和便利方面的確更好了，但在內心真正放鬆這方面卻
未必一定更好。世界的確打開了，但真正把自己鬆下來，反而比以前更難。]

question.english: What kind of holidays will be popular in the future?
question.chinese: 未來甚麼樣的假期會變得流行？

source_lines.text: Topic: What kind of holidays will be popular in the future?
未來甚麼樣的假期會變得流行？

components.english: I think holidays that help people truly switch off and recharge will become increasingly popular in the future.
components.chinese: 我認為，未來那些真正能讓人抽離日常、重新充電的假期，會愈來愈受歡迎。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: I think holidays that help people truly switch off and recharge will
become increasingly popular in the future.
[我認為，未來那些真正能讓人抽離日常、重新充電的假期，會愈來愈受歡迎。]

components.english: The main reason is that modern life is fast, noisy, and digitally crowded, so many people are no longer looking for a holiday that leaves them more tired than before. Instead, they want something that gives them breathing space. In that sense, future holidays may be less about rushing from one attraction to another and more about slowing down, clearing the mind, and feeling human again. That is why wellness breaks, nature trips, and digital-detox holidays are likely to strike a chord.

components.english: To be honest, many people are no longer satisfied with one-size-fits-all package tours. They want trips that match their own interests, values, and lifestyle. Some may prefer food-focused city breaks, others may go for cultural immersion, eco-friendly travel, or short trips built around a specific hobby. In other words, future holidays are likely to be shaped less by the idea of “going far away” and more by the idea of “going somewhere that fits me.”
components.chinese: 老實說，很多人已經不再滿足於那種千篇一律的旅行團。他們想要的是能夠配合自己興趣、價值觀，以及生活方式的旅程。有些人可能會偏好以美食為主題的城市短旅，有些人則會選擇文化沉浸式旅行、環保旅遊，或者圍繞某個興趣而設計的短途行程。換句話說，未來的假期很可能不再那麼由「去得夠不夠遠」來決定，而更多是由「那個地方是否適合我」來決定。

question.english: Do you think it is better to take a holiday in your own country or in a foreign country?
question.chinese: 主題：你認為去本地度假比較好，還是去外國度假比較好？

source_lines.text: Topic: Do you think it is better to take a holiday in your own country or in a foreign
country?
主題：你認為去本地度假比較好，還是去外國度假比較好？

components.english: Personally, I think it is generally better to take a holiday in a foreign country, because it gives people a stronger sense of novelty and a more complete break from everyday life.
components.chinese: 就我個人而言，我認為一般來說去外國度假會比較好，因為那樣能帶來更強的新鮮感，也能讓人更徹底地從日常生活中抽離。

source_lines.text: 1. Idea: Personally, I think it is generally better to take a holiday in a foreign
country, because it gives people a stronger sense of novelty and a more complete
break from everyday life.
[就我個人而言，我認為一般來說去外國度假會比較好，因為那樣能帶來更強的新鮮感，也
能讓人更徹底地從日常生活中抽離。]

components.english: The main reason is that when people travel abroad, everything feels a little different — the language, the food, the streets, the habits, even the rhythm of daily life. That change of scene can be refreshing in a way that is hard to recreate at home. In a sense, a foreign holiday lets people step out of their usual bubble and see the world with fresher eyes. It is not just about taking a rest; it is also about widening your horizons and coming back with a fuller mind.
components.chinese: 主要原因是，當人去到外國旅行時，很多東西都會顯得有點不一樣——語言、食物、街道、生活習慣，甚至整個日常節奏都不同。這種環境上的轉換，往往能帶來一種在本地較難完全複製的新鮮感。某程度上，去外國度假能讓人走出自己平常的生活泡泡，用更新鮮的眼光去看世界。它不只是休息那麼簡單，同時也是開闊眼界，然後帶著更豐富的感受回來。

source_lines.text: 2. Explanation: The main reason is that when people travel abroad, everything
feels a little different — the language, the food, the streets, the habits, even the
rhythm of daily life. That change of scene can be refreshing in a way that is hard
to recreate at home. In a sense, a foreign holiday lets people step out of their
usual bubble and see the world with fresher eyes. It is not just about taking a
rest; it is also about widening your horizons and coming back with a fuller
mind.
[主要原因是，當人去到外國旅行時，很多東西都會顯得有點不一樣——語言、食物、街道、
生活習慣，甚至整個日常節奏都不同。這種環境上的轉換，往往能帶來一種在本地較難完全
複製的新鮮感。某程度上，去外國度假能讓人走出自己平常的生活泡泡，用更新鮮的眼光去
看世界。它不只是休息那麼簡單，同時也是開闊眼界，然後帶著更豐富的感受回來。]

components.english: For example, if someone from Hong Kong spends a few days in Japan, South Korea, or Thailand, even a short trip can feel like a real reset. The food tastes different, the pace of life changes, and even simple things like taking the train or walking through a local market can feel interesting. That kind of experience tends to stay with people longer than an ordinary break close to home.
components.chinese: 例如，如果一個來自香港的人去日本、韓國，或者泰國旅行幾天，即使只是短短一趟，也往往會有一種真正重新充電的感覺。食物味道不同了，生活節奏也變了，甚至連搭車、逛當地市場這些簡單的小事，也會變得很有趣。這種經歷通常會比一次離家不遠的普通休息，更長久地留在人的記憶裡。

source_lines.text: 3. Example: For example, if someone from Hong Kong spends a few days in

experience tends to stay with people longer than an ordinary break close to
home.

久地留在人的記憶裡。]

components.english: So, if I had to choose, I would say a foreign holiday is usually better, because it offers both relaxation and a stronger sense of discovery.
components.chinese: 所以，如果一定要選一邊，我會說去外國度假通常會更好，因為它同時帶來放鬆，以及更強的探索感。

source_lines.text: 4. Conclusion: So, if I had to choose, I would say a foreign holiday is usually
better, because it offers both relaxation and a stronger sense of discovery.
[所以，如果一定要選一邊，我會說去外國度假通常會更好，因為它同時帶來放鬆，以及更
強的探索感。]

components.english: That said, I do not think holidays in your own country should be underestimated, because they can be easier, cheaper, and far less stressful.
components.chinese: 不過，我也不認為本地度假應該被低估，因為它很多時會更方便、更便宜，也更少壓力。

source_lines.text: 5. Idea 2: That said, I do not think holidays in your own country should be
underestimated, because they can be easier, cheaper, and far less stressful.
[不過，我也不認為本地度假應該被低估，因為它很多時會更方便、更便宜，也更少壓力。]

components.english: To be honest, not everyone wants a holiday that comes with flights, planning, language barriers, or the risk of things going wrong. Sometimes people are not looking for adventure; they are simply looking for peace. In that case, staying in your own country can make a lot of sense. You do not always need to cross a border to clear your head. A good local holiday can still feel like a breath of fresh air, especially if life has been hectic.
components.chinese: 老實說，並不是每個人都想要那種伴隨航班安排、行程規劃、語言障礙，甚至各種變數的假期。有時候，人想要的不是冒險，而只是平靜。在那種情況下，留在自己地方度假其實很合理。要讓自己清空腦袋，並不一定非要跨過國境不可。一個安排得好的本地假期，仍然可以像一口新鮮空氣一樣，特別是當生活一直都很忙亂的時候。

source_lines.text: 6. Explanation: To be honest, not everyone wants a holiday that comes with
flights, planning, language barriers, or the risk of things going wrong.
Sometimes people are not looking for adventure; they are simply looking for
peace. In that case, staying in your own country can make a lot of sense. You do
not always need to cross a border to clear your head. A good local holiday can
still feel like a breath of fresh air, especially if life has been hectic.
[老實說，並不是每個人都想要那種伴隨航班安排、行程規劃、語言障礙，甚至各種變數的
假期。有時候，人想要的不是冒險，而只是平靜。在那種情況下，留在自己地方度假其實很
合理。要讓自己清空腦袋，並不一定非要跨過國境不可。一個安排得好的本地假期，仍然可
以像一口新鮮空氣一樣，特別是當生活一直都很忙亂的時候。]

components.english: For instance, someone in Hong Kong could spend a weekend in Sai Kung, Cheung Chau, or a nice hotel on the waterfront and still feel properly rested. There is no long-haul travel, no complicated planning, and no need to worry about adapting to a new place. Sometimes that simplicity is exactly what people need.
components.chinese: 例如，一個在香港生活的人，可以去西貢、長洲，或者住一晚海旁酒店過週末，同樣能得到很好的休息。那樣沒有長途交通、沒有複雜的安排，也不需要花力氣去適應一個全新的地方。有時候，這種簡單本身，正正就是人最需要的東西。

source_lines.text: 7. Example: For instance, someone in Hong Kong could spend a weekend in Sai
Kung, Cheung Chau, or a nice hotel on the waterfront and still feel properly
rested. There is no long-haul travel, no complicated planning, and no need to
worry about adapting to a new place. Sometimes that simplicity is exactly what
people need.
[例如，一個在香港生活的人，可以去西貢、長洲，或者住一晚海旁酒店過週末，同樣能得到

components.english: Overall, I would say travelling to a foreign country is better if people want excitement, novelty, and a broader experience, whereas staying in their own country is better if they want convenience and a more restful break. So, in the end, the better choice depends on whether a person needs a change of world or simply a change of pace.
components.chinese: 總結來說，如果一個人想要的是新鮮感、刺激感，以及更開闊的體驗，那麼去外國度假會更好；相反，如果他想要的是方便和真正安穩的休息，那麼在自己地方度假就可能更適合。所以說到底，哪一種更好，其實取決於一個人此刻需要的，是換一個世界，還是換一個節奏。
